title: Brined Baked Chicken Breast for Caesar Salad
description: Simple brined and seasoned chicken breast, baked at high heat for juicy, flavourful slices over a kale Caesar salad. Quick brine makes all the difference.
ingredients: 4 boneless skinless chicken breasts (approx 6-7 oz each)
instructions: Dissolve 1 tbsp salt in 4 cups cold water. Submerge chicken breasts and brine for 20-30 minutes at room temperature or up to 2 hours in the fridge.
Remove chicken from brine and pat very dry with paper towels. Discard brine.
If any breasts are thicker than 1 inch, butterfly them or pound to even thickness so they cook evenly.
Rub chicken with 1 tbsp olive oil, then season with 1 tsp garlic powder, 1 tsp smoked paprika, ½ tsp black pepper, and ¼ tsp salt.
Place on a lined baking sheet and bake for 18-22 minutes until internal temperature reaches 160°F.
Remove from oven, tent loosely with foil, and rest for 5 minutes. Carryover cooking brings it to 165°F.
Slice against the grain on a slight bias for wide, even pieces over the salad. For kid quesadillas, dice instead.
notes: Macros are per breast assuming ~7 oz raw weight. The brine adds negligible sodium to the final product — most stays in the water. If you're meal prepping, brine and season the night before, then bake straight from the fridge (add 2-3 minutes to cook time). Sweet potato wedges bake at the same temp — toss them in 5 minutes before the chicken so everything finishes together.
